title: Debt relief for churches and non-profits approved
description: MOSSEL BAY NEWS - At its open monthly...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - At its open monthly meeting on Thursday 30 October, the Mossel Bay Council approved a motion of exigency brought forward by Mark Furness, portfolio chair for finance, paving the way for a special debt relief initiative aimed at supporting churches, public benefit organisations (PBOs), non-profit organisations (NPOs) and parsonages.","html":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - At its open monthly meeting on Thursday 30 October, the Mossel Bay Council approved a motion of exigency brought forward by Mark Furness, portfolio chair for finance, paving the way for a special debt relief initiative aimed at supporting churches, public benefit organisations (PBOs), non-profit organisations (NPOs) and parsonages."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The resolution not only offers significant financial relief, but also serves as a practical means of recovering outstanding municipal debt rather than writing it off completely."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This means that from 1 November to 31 January, qualifying organisations will have the opportunity to pay only 10% of their total outstanding balance (including arrears, interest and current charges).","html":"This means that from 1 November to 31 January, qualifying organisations will have the opportunity to pay only 10% of their total outstanding balance (including arrears, interest and current charges)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The remaining 90% will be written off.","html":"The remaining 90% will be written off."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Why this offer was introduced","html":"Why this offer was introduced"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Mossel Bay Municipality's spokesperson, Cornelle Carstens, says many of these institutions play a vital role in serving their communities, but often depend heavily on donations.","html":"The Mossel Bay Municipality's spokesperson, Cornelle Carstens, says many of these institutions play a vital role in serving their communities, but often depend heavily on donations."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"\"Ongoing financial pressures have made it increasingly difficult for them to settle municipal accounts.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The municipality plans to promote the offer widely to ensure all eligible organisations are informed and able to benefit from the relief programme.","html":"The municipality plans to promote the offer widely to ensure all eligible organisations are informed and able to benefit from the relief programme."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Debt relief for churches and non-profits approved
